data.title: Raffles Interior Again Delays EGM Amid Governance Probe and Audit Risks
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Raffles Interior Ltd. ( (HK:1376) ) has issued an announcement.

Raffles Interior Limited has again postponed its extraordinary general meeting, originally set for 20 March 2026, to 18 September 2026 under its articles allowing directors to delay a meeting they deem inappropriate or impracticable. The board cited the company’s deteriorating financial position and the risk that replacing all four existing directors with nominees of controlling shareholder Han Vision, while an independent investigation into Mr. Zheng is ongoing, could halt the probe, jeopardise the completion of the audit and prolong trading suspension, and it also reset the share register closure to 15–18 September 2026 to align with the new meeting date.

The directors argued that proceeding with the vote before the investigation is completed would force shareholders to make fundamental governance decisions without full information and could undermine efforts to address internal control and corporate governance breaches. Legal advisers in the Cayman Islands have opined on the legality of the postponement, and the board has left open the option of bringing the meeting forward if the investigation is finished and findings are disclosed before the new September deadline.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1376) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.12 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Raffles Interior Ltd. stock, see the HK:1376 Stock Forecast page.

More about Raffles Interior Ltd.

Raffles Interior Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, with its sole operations based in Singapore. The group operates in the interior solutions sector, and its controlling shareholder is Han Vision, ultimately owned by Mr. Zheng and his spouse, who are currently subject to an independent investigation into governance and conduct issues.

Average Trading Volume: 2,362,612

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$135M

Liu Chong Hing Investment Limited ( (HK:0194) ) has provided an update.

Liu Chong Hing Investment Limited has proposed a final ordinary cash dividend of HKD 0.17 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with the payout denominated and settled in Hong Kong dollars. The distribution remains subject to shareholder approval at the annual general meeting scheduled for 21 May 2026, aligning the company with typical Hong Kong market practices of returning capital to investors through regular dividends.

Subject to approval, the shares will trade ex-dividend on 26 May 2026, with the register of members closing from 28 May to 1 June 2026 to determine entitlements based on the 1 June 2026 record date. Payment is slated for 8 June 2026, providing investors with a clear timetable for the cash return and reinforcing visibility over shareholder remuneration in the coming year.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0194) stock is a Hold with a HK$4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Liu Chong Hing Investment Limited stock, see the HK:0194 Stock Forecast page.

The company is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a conventional corporate governance structure for a mid-sized Hong Kong issuer.

data.title: China Water Industry Flags Narrower 2025 Loss Amid Cost Cuts and Renewable Headwinds
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

China Water Industry Group Limited ( (HK:1129) ) has provided an update.

China Water Industry Group has warned that it expects to post a net loss attributable to shareholders of not more than HK$300 million for 2025, narrowing slightly from the HK$322.28 million loss recorded in 2024. The improvement is mainly driven by tighter cost controls, lower administrative and selling expenses, reduced expected credit loss provisions, lower income tax expenses and a swing to other operating income after prior-year write-offs and litigation-related costs.

The company also reported lower impairment losses on non-financial assets after substantial write-downs of discontinued or halted renewable energy projects in 2024, but these benefits were partly offset by weaker revenue and gross profit from its renewable energy business as fewer landfill sites remained in operation. Results were further dragged by the absence of a one-off gain from the 2024 disposal of Yichun Water Industry Group Company Limited and an impairment loss on deposits for acquiring more interests in a subsidiary, leaving investors cautioned as final audited 2025 figures remain subject to adjustment.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1129) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.31 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Water Industry Group Limited stock, see the HK:1129 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Water Industry Group Limited

China Water Industry Group Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the water and environmental services sector, with additional exposure to renewable energy projects such as landfill-power generation. The group’s portfolio includes water-related utilities and associated infrastructure, with revenues also tied to its renewable energy business performance.

Average Trading Volume: 289,008

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$265.5M

An announcement from Kinetic Mines & Energy Ltd. ( (HK:1277) ) is now available.

Kinetic Development Group Limited, a Hong Kong-listed coal producer incorporated in the Cayman Islands, derives most of its earnings from its core coal operations while gradually building out non-core businesses. The group emphasizes cost control, operational efficiency and safe production as it pursues expansion in coal markets at home and overseas.

The company has issued a profit warning, saying its net profit after tax for 2025 is expected to fall by 50% to 60% from 2024 amid market volatility and lower coal prices. Management cited a 40% to 45% profit decline in its core coal business, non-cash impairment provisions of roughly RMB250 million to RMB290 million, and RMB80 million to RMB90 million in losses from early-stage non-core businesses, while stressing that capital remains sufficient and the annual results will be released on 24 March 2026.

China Tourism Group Duty Free Corporation Limited Class H ( (HK:1880) ) has shared an update.

China Tourism Group Duty Free Corporation Limited has completed the acquisition of DFS Group’s Greater China retail business, excluding the DFS Hong Kong Canton Road (Sun Arcade) store, for an estimated consideration of about USD293.8 million, subject to closing adjustments. The transaction expands the company’s footprint in Greater China’s travel retail market and is expected to strengthen its competitive position in the regional duty-free and luxury retail segment.

In tandem with the acquisition, the company has issued new H shares to Delphine SAS, an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of LVMH, and to Shoppers Holdings HK Limited, linked to the Miller Family, at HKD77.21 per share. These subscriptions, representing roughly 0.43% of the enlarged share capital, bring in strategic and financial investors while slightly diluting existing shareholders, and may deepen partnerships with global luxury stakeholders.

data.title: Liu Chong Hing Investment Swings Back to Profit on Revenue Growth and Property Gains
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Liu Chong Hing Investment Limited ( (HK:0194) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Liu Chong Hing Investment Limited reported audited consolidated results for the year ended 31 December 2025, showing a sharp turnaround from a heavy loss in 2024 to a modest profit attributable to shareholders of HK$12.0 million, or HK$0.03 per share. Revenue rose to HK$1.05 billion from HK$933.0 million, driven by higher income from contracts with customers and rental operations, while finance costs declined and the group’s joint ventures swung back to profit.

Despite higher administrative and operating expenses, the company moved from a loss before tax of HK$865.7 million in 2024 to a much smaller loss before tax of HK$14.1 million in 2025, aided by reduced other losses and improved joint venture contributions. Total comprehensive income attributable to owners reached HK$168.9 million, supported by revaluation gains on properties and favorable exchange differences, signaling a significant recovery in the group’s overall financial position and offering a more stable outlook for shareholders and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0194) stock is a Hold with a HK$4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Liu Chong Hing Investment Limited stock, see the HK:0194 Stock Forecast page.

More about Liu Chong Hing Investment Limited

Liu Chong Hing Investment Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated investment holding company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group is engaged in property-related activities, including rental income from investment properties, and also earns revenue from contracts with customers as well as interest and dividend income, reflecting a diversified portfolio of real estate and financial investments.

Fangzhou Inc. reported a strong operational year in 2025, with revenue rising 30.2% to RMB3.53 billion as its consumer-facing healthcare platform continued to scale. The group recorded a net profit of RMB12.0 million, reversing a substantial loss in 2024, while adjusted net profit also improved and net assets increased, indicating a more resilient balance sheet and improved profitability.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Sinopharm Group Co ( (HK:1099) ) has issued an update.

Sinopharm Group has disclosed the 2025 audited financial results of its Shanghai-listed subsidiary China National Medicines Corporation, showing modest top-line growth but largely flat profitability. Revenue rose 3.7% year on year to RMB 52.47 billion, while net profit attributable to shareholders slipped 0.18% to RMB 1.997 billion, and earnings per share were essentially unchanged.

Despite the slight earnings decline, SINOPHARM CNCM improved its cash generation, with net operating cash flow up 20.88% versus 2024 and net assets attributable to shareholders increasing 8.27%. The weighted average return on net assets fell by nearly one percentage point, indicating some margin pressure even as total assets expanded 5.03%, suggesting steady but moderating profitability for stakeholders in a growing asset base.

The latest update is out from Asiasec Properties Limited ( (HK:0271) ).

Asiasec Properties Limited, a Hong Kong-based property company, generates revenue mainly from rental income, building management fees and associated property services tied to its investment property portfolio. The group’s operations are driven by the performance and fair value of its property assets, alongside ongoing cost management in areas such as staff, repairs and maintenance.

For the year ended 31 December 2025, Asiasec reported revenue growth to HK$50.3 million but saw its net loss widen sharply to HK$214.2 million, largely due to a substantial HK$206.7 million loss from the change in fair value of its investment properties. Despite lower finance costs and an income tax credit, higher repairs, maintenance and other operating expenses, combined with negative fair value movements, significantly deteriorated overall profitability and increased the basic loss per share to 17.26 Hong Kong cents, underscoring continued pressure on asset valuations and returns for shareholders.

An update from APAC Resources ( (HK:1104) ) is now available.

APAC Resources Limited has completed its re-domiciliation from Bermuda to Hong Kong, with the change of domicile, adoption of new articles of association and a Chinese name, and the elimination and application of contributed surplus all becoming effective on 19 March 2026. The company emphasised that the move does not create a new legal entity or disrupt business continuity but is intended to strengthen its strategic presence in Hong Kong and bolster investor confidence, while its Hong Kong share registrar remains unchanged and its registered office has been formally shifted to Wanchai.

Following the re-domicile, APAC Resources will proceed to apply for deregistration in Bermuda, signalling a full transition of its corporate home to Hong Kong’s jurisdiction. The board also cautioned shareholders, warrantholders and potential investors to exercise care when dealing in the company’s securities during this period of structural change, underscoring the ongoing nature of the implementation process and associated regulatory steps.

An update from Onewo, Inc. Class H ( (HK:2602) ) is now available.

Onewo Inc., a PRC domestic enterprise listed in Hong Kong under stock code 02602, focuses on serving a broad base of H share investors, including both institutional and individual holders located outside mainland China. As an equity issuer, it manages its shareholder base through Tricor Investor Services in Hong Kong and adheres to local and PRC tax and regulatory requirements in handling distributions.

The company has proposed a final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 0.546 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, subject to shareholder approval on 8 May 2026. data.title: Infinity Logistics Delays 2025 Results and Faces Trading Suspension After Auditor Change
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited ( (HK:1442) ).

Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited has warned that it will miss the 31 March 2026 deadline for publishing its audited annual results for the year ended 31 December 2025, after the late appointment of new auditor Nexia in February created additional time pressure for completing key audit procedures. The group says it is prioritising completion of the audit over issuing unaudited management accounts to avoid confusing shareholders and expects to release preliminary 2025 results on 9 April and dispatch its annual report by 30 April, with a board meeting scheduled for 9 April to approve the accounts and consider a final dividend.

As a consequence of the delay, trading in the company’s shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange will be suspended from 1 April 2026 and will only resume once the 2025 results are published, a move that temporarily restricts liquidity and heightens caution for investors. The board says it is not aware of any other material audit issues beyond the timing impact of the auditor change, but has urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise care in dealing in the company’s securities during the suspension period.

data.title: Road King Flags Sharply Wider 2025 Loss on Prolonged Property Slump
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Road King Infrastructure ( (HK:1098) ) has provided an announcement.

Road King Infrastructure has warned that it expects to post a significantly wider net loss for the year ended 31 December 2025, projecting a group net loss of HK$5.2 billion to HK$5.5 billion and a loss attributable to shareholders of HK$5.8 billion to HK$6.1 billion. The deterioration from 2024 results is mainly driven by a prolonged slump in the property market and a harsh operating environment that have eroded margins on property projects in Mainland China and Hong Kong and forced higher impairment charges on properties and related assets.

The company noted that last year’s smaller loss was partly cushioned by a substantial one-off gain from the disposal of its entire toll road business in Mainland China, a benefit that will not recur in 2025, thereby exposing the full extent of operational weakness in its core property portfolio. The figures are based on unaudited management estimates, and the company has urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution in trading its shares ahead of the formal annual results announcement expected in late March 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1098) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Road King Infrastructure stock, see the HK:1098 Stock Forecast page.

More about Road King Infrastructure

Road King Infrastructure Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in Bermuda that operates through subsidiaries across Mainland China and Hong Kong. The group has historically been involved in infrastructure-related businesses, including toll roads, and currently has significant exposure to property development projects in these markets, leaving it sensitive to fluctuations in regional real estate conditions.
